data.epi_content:
I stole a body.

Ah, to be precise, perhaps I stole a corpse.

Maybe that's why I'm being punished.

Because I'm trapped inside a human who's destined to die in a few days.

The problem is that I'm still alive.

What kind of goddamn novel is this.

If this is inside a novel, I'd put a gun in my mouth to return to my shitty reality.

The hard-as-hell gun barrel.

Yet it's disgustingly short like a tiny Vietcong, too small to even reach down my throat—that worthless black barrel.

Those black folks who work well while being whipped on farms—I just hope this thing works well too.

Lately, it feels like my sense of self is getting all mixed up in my head, which is rather unpleasant.

Each time I spin the barrel, it makes a click-clack, click-clack sound.
data.epi_content2:
With only one bullet inside, my chances of dying are just one in six.

Should I put a finger on the trigger?

I'm shaking so damn hard.

Enough to make my teeth ache.

"Hoo, right, fu...ck. Fuck. That's it."

This lady's mouth seems to have only been accustomed to refined language, making it difficult to even curse properly.

If I pull the trigger now, I'll just end up with a hole in the back of my neck, and that's it.

I might die in excruciating pain, or someone might treat me and I might not die at all.

I raised the gun so the barrel grazed the roof of my mouth, held the trigger with my left hand, and roughly aimed at my head with my right.

Then, closing my eyes.
data.epi_content3:
The trigger...

Click.

No gunshot came.

Checking inside, I realized two more pulls and I might have scattered my brains across the sky.

"Tastes disgustingly stale."

With a slightly bitter, metallic taste of gunpowder, I spit out the saliva pooled in my mouth onto the floor.

I tossed the gun I was holding onto the desk and rubbed my face.

Every day is hell.

Especially when there's no way out.
